edges.target: Alveolar surfactant and proteinaceous material accumulation

nodes.description: Unprocessed surfactant lipids and proteins accumulate in alveolar spaces, creating proteinaceous material, foamy macrophages, crazy-paving HRCT, gas exchange impairment, hypoxemia, and respiratory failure in severe disease.
nodes.id: Alveolar surfactant and proteinaceous material accumulation

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (Orphanet structured-database record)

evidence.snippet: Pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (PAP) is a rare disorder characterized by the accumulation of surfactant lipids and protein in the alveolar spaces, with resultant impairment in gas exchange.

nodes.description: Reported CSF2RA alleles include missense, duplication, frameshift, nonsense, exon or gene deletion, and splicing abnormalities that abolish or severely reduce GM-CSF receptor alpha-chain function.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary pulmonary alveolar proteinosis: pathogenesis, presentation, diagnosis, and therapy.
evidence.snippet: Eight children (seven female, one male) were identified with PAP caused by recessive CSF2RA mutations.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary pulmonary alveolar proteinosis: pathogenesis, presentation, diagnosis, and therapy.
evidence.snippet: Genetic analysis revealed multiple distinct CSF2RA abnormalities, including missense, duplication, frameshift, and nonsense mutations; exon and gene deletion; and cryptic alternative splicing.

nodes.description: Homozygous CSF2RB variants can impair GM-CSF receptor beta-chain expression and downstream signaling, producing hereditary PAP.

evidence.reference_title: Adult-onset hereditary pulmonary alveolar proteinosis caused by a single-base deletion in CSF2RB.
evidence.snippet: Genetic screening revealed a homozygous, single-base deletion at nt 631 in exon 6 of CSF2RB on chromosome 22, which caused reductions in GM-CSF dependent signalling and function.

nodes.description: Germline pathogenic variants in CSF2RA or CSF2RB disrupt the alpha or beta subunits of the GM-CSF receptor, reducing or abolishing GM-CSF receptor signaling in myeloid cells.

biological_processes: JAK-STAT signaling downstream of GM-CSF receptor

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary pulmonary alveolar proteinosis: pathogenesis, presentation, diagnosis, and therapy.
evidence.snippet: Molecular analysis demonstrated that GM-CSF signaling was absent in six and severely reduced in two patients.

evidence.reference_title: Adult-onset hereditary pulmonary alveolar proteinosis caused by a single-base deletion in CSF2RB.
evidence.snippet: Disruption of granulocyte/macrophage colony-stimulating factor (GM-CSF) signalling causes pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (PAP).

nodes.description: The HRCT crazy-paving pattern reflects ground-glass opacities with septal thickening.
nodes.id: Crazy paving pattern on pulmonary HRCT

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0025391 | Crazy paving pattern on pulmonary HRCT | Frequent (79-30%)
high-resolution computed tomography reveals diffuse ground-glass opacities and airspace consolidation with interlobular septal thickening in a characteristic "crazy paving" pattern.

nodes.description: Impaired GM-CSF-dependent macrophage function reduces cholesterol and surfactant clearance from alveoli, producing dysfunctional alveolar macrophages and progressive surfactant retention.

evidence.snippet: Pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (PAP) is a syndrome characterized by the accumulation of alveolar surfactant and dysfunction of alveolar macrophages.
reduced GM-CSF-dependent cholesterol clearance in alveolar macrophages, which impairs alveolar surfactant clearance.

nodes.description: Pulmonary macrophage transplantation or gene-corrected macrophage approaches are experimental strategies intended to replace defective alveolar macrophage function in hereditary PAP.

evidence.reference_title: iPSC-Derived Macrophages Effectively Treat Pulmonary Alveolar Proteinosis in Csf2rb-Deficient Mice.
evidence.snippet: PMT significantly improves alveolar protein deposition and other critical herPAP disease parameters.

nodes.description: Foamy alveolar macrophages reflect surfactant and lipid accumulation.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (Orphanet structured-database record)

nodes.description: Hypoxemia reflects impaired gas exchange from alveolar surfactant accumulation.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (Orphanet structured-database record)

nodes.description: Severe hereditary PAP can progress to respiratory failure requiring ventilatory support.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0004887 | Respiratory failure requiring assisted ventilation | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Whole-lung lavage mechanically removes excess surfactant and proteinaceous material from the alveoli and remains a main symptomatic treatment for PAP, including CSF2RA-associated hereditary PAP.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary pulmonary alveolar proteinosis: pathogenesis, presentation, diagnosis, and therapy.
evidence.snippet: All symptomatic patients responded well to whole-lung lavage therapy.
The standard of care for treatment of PAP remains whole lung lavage
